task_context.description: This is a multi-objective detector design optimization task that involves simulating three different electromagnetic calorimeter configurations with varying cost-performance trade-offs. The task requires running Geant4 simulations to measure energy resolution, mass, and shower containment for each design, then calculating cost estimates using provided material pricing models. The unique aspect is the explicit cost-benefit analysis to find the optimal resolution-per-dollar ratio, combining physics performance metrics with economic constraints. Each configuration represents a different point on the cost-performance spectrum, from premium crystal calorimeters to budget sampling designs.
task_context.goal: Find the electromagnetic calorimeter configuration that provides the best energy resolution per dollar spent
constraints: Target ~20 radiation lengths total depth for all designs
Use electrons (e-) in Geant4 particle gun
Energy sweep limited to [1, 5, 20] GeV for tractable simulation
Must measure energy resolution, total mass, material cost, and shower containment for each design

observations.content: Step completed successfully: Generate geometry for premium PbWO4 crystal calorimeter with ~20 radiation lengths depth
Step completed successfully: Generate geometry for midrange lead-scintillator sampling calorimeter with ~20 radiation lengths depth
Step completed successfully: Generate geometry for budget iron-scintillator sampling calorimeter with ~20 radiation lengths depth
Step completed successfully: Run electron energy sweep simulation for PbWO4 calorimeter across all energy points
Step completed successfully: Run electron energy sweep simulation for lead-scintillator calorimeter across all energy points
Step completed successfully: Run electron energy sweep simulation for iron-scintillator calorimeter across all energy points
Step completed successfully: Analyze energy resolution for PbWO4 calorimeter and fit stochastic parameters
Step completed successfully: Analyze energy resolution for lead-scintillator calorimeter and fit stochastic parameters
Step completed successfully: Analyze energy resolution for iron-scintillator calorimeter and fit stochastic parameters
Step completed successfully: Calculate resolution-per-dollar figure of merit and identify optimal cost-performance configuration
Step completed successfully: Create comprehensive comparison plots of energy resolution vs energy for all configurations

observations.content: Step completed successfully: Generate comprehensive cost-benefit analysis report with optimization recommendations

data.output:
{'report': '# Electromagnetic Calorimeter Cost-Performance Optimization Report

## Executive Summary

This study successfully completed a comprehensive multi-objective optimization of electromagnetic calorimeter designs, evaluating three distinct configurations across the cost-performance spectrum. All primary objectives were achieved, including Geant4 simulations, performance analysis, and cost-benefit optimization to identify the optimal energy resolution per dollar ratio.

## Task Overv
